title: Cells, Walls, Passages, Boundaries, and Invariants · Language Clinic: Reciprocal Passage
summary: One cell opens east while its neighbor stays closed west, boundary walls disappear, and rendering data becomes the maze source of truth. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the reciprocal passage increment in reciprocal wall and passage model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Carve a passage as one atomic reciprocal update between adjacent cells.
Represent north, east, south, and west walls or passage edges with one documented ownership policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-reciprocal-passage — Carve a passage as one atomic reciprocal update between adjacent cells.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-wall-cell-invariants-3
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-wall-cell-invariants-3.
# Case 5798: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def worked_maze_wall_cell_invariants_3(cells: int = 48, reciprocal_openings: int = 47, passage_edges: int = 47, boundary_breaches: int = 0, illegal_diagonals: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "gridworked3",
        "topology5798",
        "search3dekrow",
        "eventworked35798",
        "access5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or passage_edges < 0:
        return False, passage_edges, "topology-count"
    if reciprocal_openings != passage_edges:
        return False, reciprocal_openings, "reciprocal-wall"
    if boundary_breaches > 0:
        return False, boundary_breaches, "boundary-policy"
    if illegal_diagonals > 0:
        return False, illegal_diagonals, "illegal-neighbor"
    assert reciprocal_openings == passage_edges
    return True, passage_edges, "valid-wall-model"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-reciprocal-passage-dj9c5c
# Competency: maze-reciprocal-passage.
# Case 8247: a corrupted-state recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def evidence_maze_reciprocal_passage_dj9c5c(cells: int = 48, reciprocal_openings: int = 47, passage_edges: int = 47, boundary_breaches: int = 0, illegal_diagonals: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_dj9c5c = "dj9c5c-c5c9jd-8247"
    assert evidence_variant_dj9c5c
    evidence_axes_dj9c5c = (
        "griddj9c5c",
        "topology8247",
        "searchc5c9jd",
        "eventdj9c5c8247",
        "access8247dj9c5c",
        "recoveryc5c9jd8247",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_dj9c5c)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or passage_edges < 0:
        return False, passage_edges, "topology-count"
    if reciprocal_openings != passage_edges:
        return False, reciprocal_openings, "reciprocal-wall"
    if boundary_breaches > 0:
        return False, boundary_breaches, "boundary-policy"
    if illegal_diagonals > 0:
        return False, illegal_diagonals, "illegal-neighbor"
    assert reciprocal_openings == passage_edges
    return True, passage_edges, "valid-wall-model"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Carve a passage as one atomic reciprocal update between adjacent cells.

Build deterministic Python evidence to validate reciprocal passages, boundary policy, and model topology before rendering; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
